onboarding.title: Critterboard
onboarding.tagline: id any bug · stay offline · be smug

scan.permissionAsk: Tap allow to let Critterboard see the bug. Photos never leave your device.
scan.permissionDenied: Camera was denied. Enable it in Settings → Critterboard → Camera.

disambiguate.foot: ID ran fully on-device · 320 ms · no photo left your phone

noMatch.heroSub: BugNet v3 returned all candidates below 15%.

tip.lightDesc: Move into the sun or use the flash. Shadow eats detail.
tip.frameDesc: Get within 20 cm and crop tight on the body.
tip.backdropDesc: Less foliage clutter helps the model focus.
tip.profileDesc: Wings and legs visible from the side are easiest.

noMatch.saveDesc: Stash the photo in your journal. Re-ID it later when models improve.

dex.title: My Dex
dex.emptyDesc: Try fewer letters, a latin name, or a rarity ({rarities}).
dex.emptyHint: Tip: prefix with ? to search uncaught slots by number (e.g. ?7)

activity.title: Buzz
activity.sub: {n} new since you opened the app

item.a3Sub: Catch one bug today. Even a hoverfly.
item.a8Title: wingedfren replied to your hoverfly thread
item.a8Sub: "Pretty sure that's a Marmalade — wing bands."

friends.title: Naturalists
friends.emptySub: Check the Suggested tab to find naturalists like you.

leaderboard.title: Leaderboard
leaderboard.hiddenDesc: Turn on "Show me on leaderboard" to appear.

permissions.title: Three quick asks.
permissions.sub: You can change any of these later in Brains.

camera.title: Camera
camera.desc: Snap a bug, get an ID. Photos never leave your device.

location.title: Location
location.desc: Better range filtering — the model knows what lives near you.
location.b2: Stays on device unless you share

notifs.title: Notifications
notifs.desc: Daily streak nudge + Bug of the Day. We'll never spam.
notifs.b3: Off by default after 7 days idle

q1.desc: Pollinators keep the world fed. Find and ID three of them today — bees, butterflies, hoverflies, beetles, anything that visits a flower for a snack.
q1.tip1: Honeybees count, hoverflies count, even beetles on flowers count.
q1.tip2: Morning sun + a flowering patch is the cheat code.

q2.desc: Beetles. Crunchy. Misunderstood. Bag yourself any species in order Coleoptera.
q2.tip1: Flip a log (gently) — most beetles love the dark.
q2.tip2: Try the underside of leaves at dusk.

q3.desc: A real trophy. Track down a Legendary-tier insect this week. Luna Moth, Atlas Moth, or anything wearing five stars.
q3.tip1: Legendaries cluster at dusk near porch lights.
q3.tip2: Check the Map for recent rare sightings nearby.

q4.desc: ID at least one bug a day for seven days in a row. Doesn't matter what — a single ant counts.
q4.tip1: Missed a day? Toughens you up. Start the count again.
q4.tip2: Set a reminder when your streak hits 5 — the home stretch is brutal.

b1.name: First Catch
b1.desc: Your very first ID. Welcome to the club — every naturalist has this one tucked in the front of their notebook.

b2.name: Streak 3
b2.desc: Three days of paying attention in a row. The hard part is starting; the harder part is showing up tomorrow.
b2.crit: ID at least one bug, three days running.

b3.name: Night Owl
b3.desc: You went out after dark with a flashlight and found something looking back. Moths salute you.

b4.name: Pollinator Pal
b4.desc: For the friends of flowers. Bees, butterflies, hoverflies, beetles — anything that's there for the nectar.

b5.name: Splitter
b5.desc: You can tell a hoverfly from a wasp at twenty paces. Lumpers fear you.

b6.name: Centurion
b6.desc: One hundred IDs. The kind of milestone that gets a little gold ribbon on it.

b7.desc: Locked. Keep hunting and this one will reveal itself.

b8.desc: Locked. Keep hunting and this one will reveal itself.

streak.freezeDesc: Auto-uses on a missed day so your streak survives. Earn one every 7 days.

larva.idle: Point the phone at the noise. I'll do my actual job.
larva.matched: Top match locked. Don't say I never did anything for you.
larva.listening: Filtering out your breathing. You breathe a lot.

snail.idle: Hold the phone steady. A few seconds of clean audio is enough.
snail.listening: Listening carefully. The pattern is emerging.

maywind.idle: Ooh ooh — let's hear that buzz!
maywind.matched: NAILED IT! Look at that signal!
maywind.listening: Picking up something cool — keep going!

settings.title: On-device Brains
settings.sub: Everything runs on your phone. Promise.
settings.profileLeaks: Some data may leave your device.
settings.nameHint: Shown on the leaderboard and shared sightings.
settings.locShareOn: Pins added to the public map (±500m fuzz).
settings.locShareOff: Your map stays private to this device.
settings.regionsSub: Boosts ID accuracy where you actually are.
settings.regionFoot: Packs run fully offline. Tap an installed pack to see what's inside.
settings.languageSub: For UI, species names, and {name}'s sass.
settings.creditsTitle: Free forever. No ads. No tracking.
settings.creditsSub: Built with love by 3 people who really like beetles.

na-ne.tagline: Hardwood forests, beaver ponds, salt marsh.
notes: Salt-marsh species weighted higher than inland average.
Spring–summer phenology baked into the prior; off-season IDs may drop ~6%.
Native-bee fauna oversampled vs. honey bees.
Desert ant taxonomy still in flux — confidence capped at 92%.

larva.name: Prof. Larva
larva.title: larva-3b · deadpan
larva.blurb: Snarky. Helpful in spite of itself.

lines.intro: "Oh great, another trainer. Let me drop everything."
lines.scanTip: Yeah yeah, focus the camera. I'm an AI, not a magician.
lines.streak: "Three days in a row. Your therapist must be thrilled."
lines.topicHello: So we're talking about the {topic} now? Fine.
lines.common: "It's a {name}. Yes. You really needed me for this?"
lines.legendary: "A {name}. Okay fine, I'm impressed. Don't tell the cicadas."

larva.uncertain: Three options. The model is, technically, vibes.
larva.noMatch: Nothing. I see leaves. Are you photographing leaves?
larva.streakSass: Don't break it. I keep score.
larva.badgeLocked: Don't get excited. You haven't earned it yet.
larva.badgeEarned: Fine. I'm a little impressed. Don't make it weird.
canned: Look. I'm doing my best. Which is more than I can say for you.
Hoverflies hover. It's literally in the name.
If it has six legs and judges you silently, it's probably an insect.
Try a clearer photo. Or therapy. Your call.
Bees are friends. Wasps are vibes. Hornets are problems.

snail.name: Dr. Snail
snail.title: snail-3b · patient

lines.intro: "Welcome. Take your time — we'll learn together."
lines.scanTip: Steady the camera. There is no rush. The bug is waiting.
lines.streak: "Four wonderful days of paying attention. Well done."
lines.chatHello: Hello, friend. What did you find today?
lines.topicHello: Ah, the {topic}. A fine choice to study. Where shall we begin?
lines.common: "A {name}. A lovely common visitor — the kind of bug that teaches you to look closely."
lines.legendary: "A {name}. Pause a moment with this one. They are not often seen."

snail.uncertain: A few candidates. Look closely — you'll know which.
snail.noMatch: No match this time. The light or the angle may be the issue — let's try again together.
snail.streakSass: A lovely rhythm. Tomorrow, again, gently.
snail.badgeLocked: Patience. It will come when it comes.
snail.badgeEarned: Well-earned. A quiet moment to be proud.
canned: A good question. Many naturalists confuse these — you are in good company.
Look closely at the wing venation. It is the most reliable clue.
Take your time. Identification is not a race.
Try again with a slightly closer crop, and we will work through it together.
Each sighting teaches us something. Even the ones we cannot place.

maywind.name: R.A. Maywind
maywind.title: maywind-3b · curious

lines.intro: "Hi hi! Notebook ready, boots laced — let's find a critter!"
lines.scanTip: Ooh ooh — point that lens! I LOVE this part.
lines.analyzing: Cross-checking the database — this is the fun bit!
lines.streak: "Four days in a row! You absolute legend!"
lines.chatHello: Hi! What are we looking at today? Tell me everything.
lines.topicHello: Ooh, a {topic}! Brilliant pick. Ask me anything!
lines.common: "A {name}! Classic crowd-pleaser. Ten out of ten, would identify again."
lines.legendary: "OH MY GOSH a {name}! I'm writing this in the notebook in BIG LETTERS."

maywind.uncertain: Ooh, a puzzle! I narrowed it to three. Help me pick!
maywind.noMatch: Hmm! Came up empty. New angle, brighter light, and we'll nail it!
maywind.streakSass: FOUR DAYS! Tomorrow makes five — you've got this!
maywind.badgeLocked: Ooh, this one's GREAT — you're gonna love unlocking it!
maywind.badgeEarned: YES! Look at you! I'm putting this in the notebook!
canned: Bugs are SO cool. Have I mentioned that yet today?
Ooh good question! Antennae shape is the secret handshake.
Try a slightly closer crop and we'll nail it on the next pass!
Field tip: bees are fuzzy, wasps are shiny, hoverflies are show-offs.
You've got the eye for this. Truly.

help.title: Help & About

faq1.a: Yes. Vision, chat, and the species database all run on your phone. The only reason to turn on Network access is to share to the leaderboard or post sightings publicly.

faq2.q: Why is my ID confidence low?
faq2.a: Usually one of three things: the photo is blurry, the bug fills less than 1/4 of the frame, or you're outside an installed regional pack. Try a closer crop, brighter light, and a side profile.

faq3.a: You earn one freeze every 7 days of activity, capped at three banked. If you miss a day, the freeze auto-spends to keep your streak alive. You don't have to do anything.

faq4.q: Can I use Critterboard if I'm colorblind?
faq4.a: Yes. All confidence and rarity cues double-encode with shape and label. There's a high-contrast pass in development; ping us if a specific screen is hurting you.

faq5.q: Where do reference images come from?
faq5.a: iNaturalist (CC-BY-NC) for photos; GBIF for ranges. We don't train on your photos — model updates ship from our side only.

faq6.q: How do I delete my data?
faq6.a: Scroll down to "Wipe everything." It nukes your dex, sightings, settings, and on-device models. Followers and leaderboard entries go too. There is no undo.

help.contactDesc: A real human reads every message. Reply usually within 48 hours.
help.dataSub: It lives on your phone. Take it with you.

data.exportUnavailable: Sharing isn't available on this device
data.wipeDesc: Dex, sightings, models, settings. No undo.

help.appSpecOn: iOS 16+ · 312 MB on disk · network ON
help.appSpecOff: iOS 16+ · 312 MB on disk · fully offline

credits.intro: Made by three people who genuinely think bugs are cool. No VC money, no ads, no tracking, no account. Just bugs and a phone.
credits.footer:
All processing happens on your device.
No data leaves this phone. We promise.
